1.description: The timing of macroeconomic risk and income insurance. Left panel: Variance ratios of value-added, wages, EBIT, and dividends as a function of the horizon. Shaded areas denote heteroskedasticity-robust 95% confidence intervals. Right panel: Variance ratios of value added, wages, EBIT, dividends, and value added minus wages (dashed lines denote heteroskedasticity-robust 95% confidence interval) as a function of the horizon. The data are annual over the period 1946 to 2013.

2.description: The term structure effect of income insurance. The standardized labor-share (solid line) is plotted together with the standardized five-year variance ratios of wages minus the variance ratios of dividends (dashed line, left panel), the five-year variance ratios of wages (middle), and the five-year variance ratios of dividends (right). The data pertain to the nonfinancial corporate sector (Flow of Funds) and are quarterly over the period 1951:4 to 2015:1. The time series of variance ratios are computed using a rolling window of 10 years.

3.description: Income insurance and the term structure of equity. The standardized labor-share from the nonfinancial corporate sector (solid line) is plotted together with the standardized one-year-ahead dividend strip return volatility (dashed line, left panel), the standardized one-year-ahead excess return over the risk-free rate (middle), and the standardized one-year-ahead excess return over the market return (right). The data are quarterly over the period 1996:4 to 2010:1.

4.description: Variance ratios and persistence. Left panel: Variance ratios of wages, consumption, and dividends as a function of the horizon. Dashed lines denote empirical data. Right panel: Autocorrelation function of the empirical and model-implied labor-share. Cash flow parameters are from TableVI.

5.description: The term structure effect of income insurance. Left panel: Variance ratios of dividends as a function of the labor-share for several horizons (τ = 2, 5, and 10 years). Right panel: The difference between the variance ratios of wages and dividends as a function of the labor-share for several horizons (τ = 2, 5, and 10 years). Cash flow parameters are from TableVI.

6.description: Term structures of dividend strips. Term structures of the dividend strip return premium, volatility, and Sharpe ratio are reported in the upper left, upper right, and lower left panel, respectively. The horizontal dashed line denotes the corresponding moment for equity. The lower right panel shows dividend strip return volatility, equity return volatility, dividend volatility, and consumption volatility. Cash flow parameters are from TableVIandγ=10,ψ=1.5, andβ=3.5%.

7.description: The horizon decomposition of the equity premium. Equilibrium densityH(t,τ)as a function of the horizon τ (i.e., the relative contribution of each horizon τ to the equity premium). Solid, dot-dashed, and dashed lines denote the baseline calibration of TableVI(ϕ=0.396) and the cases of no income insurance (ϕ=0) and augmented insurance (ϕ=3×0.396), respectively.

8.description: Bond and equity yields. Term structures of equity yields (left upper panel), real bond yields (right upper panel), expected dividend growth (left lower panel), and the premium on equity yields (right lower panel) at the steady state as a function of the horizon. Solid and dashed lines denote the baseline calibration of TableVIand the case of no income insurance (ϕ=0), respectively.

9.description: Business cycle uncertainty and term structures. Left upper panel: Term structures of the variance ratios of consumption, wages, and dividends as a function of the horizon. Dashed lines denote empirical data. Other panels: Term structures of dividend volatility (right upper panel), dividend strip return volatility (left lower panel), and dividend strip premium (right lower panel) as a function of the horizon. Solid lines denote the steady state (zt=z¯), and dashed and dot-dashed lines denote good and bad states, respectively. Cash flow parameters:σx=0.005,μ¯=0.02,λμ=0.86,σμ=0.018,z¯=0.1,λz=0.19,σz=0.026,α=0.94, andϕ=0.418. Preference parameters:γs=7.5,ψ=1.5, andβ=4.5%.
